disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Gradual painless decline in vision over about two years — blurring, glare sensitivity, difficulty reading fine print and navigating in dim light, with repeated glasses changes giving little benefit
trigger_question_examples: How long has the eyesight been getting worse?
What is the vision like in dim light or with glare?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Her eyes have been getting blurry for a couple of years now — bright lights dazzle her, she can't read small print, and she keeps needing new glasses that don't really help.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Gradual decline in hearing over about two years — difficulty hearing high-pitched sounds and understanding speech in background noise, television turned up very loud
trigger_question_examples: Does she struggle to follow conversation when there is background noise?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She can't hear us properly, especially if there's any noise around, and she has the TV up so loud the whole street can hear it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute onset this evening of severe agitation, a panic attack and a near-miss fall (tripped over an unnoticed obstacle in the hallway)
trigger_question_examples: What happened tonight that made you call?
Has she had any falls or near-falls?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Tonight she just went to pieces — panicking and agitated — and she nearly went over in the hallway because she didn't see something on the floor.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Family perceives the patient as suddenly confused / as though she has had a sudden cognitive collapse
trigger_question_examples: What do you mean she seems confused?
Is this a change from how she usually is?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She seems so confused and not herself — we're terrified she's suddenly losing her mind.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient is awake and responds and follows conversation when a person stands close and speaks up facing her — no drowsiness or waxing-and-waning alertness reported
trigger_question_examples: Is she drowsy or hard to rouse, or is she awake and alert?
When you get close and speak up facing her, does she answer you and follow what you say?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She's wide awake — if we get right up close and speak up so she can see our faces, she does answer us and follows what we're saying, she's just frightened.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new weakness of the face, arm or leg, no drooping of the face and no new slurring of speech reported
trigger_question_examples: Has one side of her face drooped, or is one arm or leg weak?
Is her speech slurred in a new way?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing like that — her face is even and her arms and legs are moving normally, and her speech isn't slurred.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No sudden complete loss of vision in one eye and no severe eye pain or red eye reported
trigger_question_examples: Did she suddenly lose all the sight in one eye?
Is either eye painful or very red?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there's been no sudden blackout in one eye and her eyes aren't painful or red — it's been slow and gradual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The near-miss fall did not involve a head strike and she is not reporting new pain or inability to bear weight; no fever or shivers reported
trigger_question_examples: Did she actually hit her head or the floor?
Can she stand and walk on it, and does she have any fever or shivers?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She caught herself and didn't hit her head or go down, she can stand and walk, and she hasn't got a fever or the shivers.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive social withdrawal and isolation because of frustration communicating
trigger_question_examples: How has this affected her day-to-day life and mood?
Has she been withdrawing or avoiding people?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She's stopped going out and doesn't want to talk to anyone anymore because she gets so frustrated not being able to hear or see.
